raw_text:
Confirmed — with one tweak to reduce wasted cycles.

✅ Confirmed plan (and why)

Your bead mapping and priorities are correct, and your execution order is directionally right.

One tweak to execution order

Move bd-nln (stale containers / runtime freshness) earlier, right after the first two schema “mass-failure” fixes.

Recommended order:

bd-15t (audit enum 23505)

bd-yp2 (AR updated_at default/fixtures)

bd-nln (fresh images / runtime policy) ← move up here

bd-2mj (GL source_module + updated_at refs)

bd-odv (outbox tables + notifications ref)

bd-1uy (type mismatches)

bd-3vl (test isolation)

bd-1q4 (payments.amount_minor + subscriptions.app_id)

Reason: you have endpoint tests failing due to stale images; if you fix GL/outbox/types while still running old containers, you’ll keep reading false signals from /api/version and readiness-related suites.

✅ Assignments

Your agent assignments are good.

CopperRiver: bd-15t, bd-nln, bd-odv
(Critical path: migration idempotency + runtime freshness + outbox existence)

PurpleCliff: bd-yp2, bd-2mj, bd-1q4
(Schema/fixture truth alignment)

MaroonHarbor: bd-1uy, bd-3vl
(Test harness strict typing + determinism)

Stop-the-line reminders (still apply)

bd-odv: don’t “fix” outbox by weakening atomicity checks. Ensure tables + migrations are real and applied.

bd-nln: must guarantee tests hit current HEAD images (SHA-tag or compose build) with a single command.

bd-3vl: isolation should be deterministic (truncate/reset per test or unique tenant/app ids), not “sleep more.”

Proceed with the tweaked order.
